steps.title: Map the time shift
steps.content: The text first describes Native fiction from the 1960s and 1970s as realistic and serious. The word however then introduces younger Native writers using popular genres such as fantasy and horror.

steps.title: Reject mismatched structures
steps.content: Choice B invents praise and criticism. Choice C adds non-Native writers, who never appear. Choice D treats the paragraph as one author's early and later works, but it contrasts generations of writers.

steps.title: Choose the generational contrast
steps.content: Choice A matches the organization: earlier Native fiction is discussed first, then more recent Native fiction is described as different in genre and tone. The answer is A.
